Not a Medium member? Read full article here.
The Problem: The “Overloaded” Prompt
When developers first try to automate workflows, they try to cram everything into one massive prompt: “Write a marketing blog post, check it for SEO, translate it into Spanish, ensure it matches our brand guidelines, and format it in Markdown.”
The result? The “Lost in the Middle” Syndrome.

• Instruction Amnesia: The LLM gets overwhelmed. It writes the post and translates it, but completely forgets to apply the brand guidelines or check the SEO.

• Brittle Workflows: If the translation step fails, the entire prompt fails, and you have to start the generation over from scratch, wasting tokens.

• No Modularity: You cannot easily swap out the “SEO instructions” without risking breaking the “Translation instructions.”
Press enter or click to view image in full size

The Solution: The Prompt Chaining Pattern
To build robust, production-grade automation, architects use Prompt Chaining. Think of this as Henry Ford’s Assembly Line for AI.
